title: pH STABLE TRIVALENT CHROMIUM COATING SOLUTIONS
abstract: A stable trivalent chromium coating solution can be prepared by heating the chromium coating solution and adjusting the pH of the chromium coating solution. The coating solution can be heated to at least 35 Â°C for no more than 24 hours. While the solution is hot, a basic or acidic material can be added to increase or reduce the pH to a target value, respectively. In some embodiments, the chromium coating solution can have a pH of 3.5 to 4.0 and the pH can vary no more than Â± 0.15 while in storage for at least 20 days. The trivalent chromium coating solution can include a fluoride compound, a zirconate compound, a corrosion inhibitor compound, a chelating agent, a phosphorous compound, and/or other compounds.
claims: Claims 1. A method of stabilizing a trivalent chromium coating solution, the method comprising: heating the trivalent chromium coating solution; and adjusting the pH of the trivalent chromium coating solution while heating the trivalent chromium coating solution. 2. The method of claim 1 comprising heating the trivalent chromium coating solution to at least 35 Â°C. 3. The method of claim 1 comprising heating the trivalent chromium coating solution for no more than 24 hours. 4. The method of claim 1 comprising adjusting the pH of the trivalent chromium coating solution to approximately 3.5 to approximately 4.0 while heating the trivalent chromium coating solution. 5. The method of claim 1 wherein adjusting the pH of the trivalent chromium coating solution comprises adding an alkaline material to the trivalent chromium coating solution to raise the pH of the trivalent chromium coating solution. 6. The method of claim 1 comprising mixing the trivalent chromium coating solution while adjusting the pH. 7. The method of claim 1 comprising filtering precipitates from the trivalent chromium coating solution after adjusting the pH. 8. The method of claim 1 comprising cooling the trivalent chromium coating solution. 9. The method of claim 8 wherein upon cooling the trivalent chromium coating solution, the pH of the trivalent chromium coating solution varies no more than + 0.15 while in storage for at least 7 days. 10. The method of claim 1 wherein the trivalent chromium coating solution is an aqueous trivalent chromium coating solution. 11. The method of claim 1 wherein the trivalent chromium coating solution is free or substantially free of hexavalent chromium. 12. The method of claim 1 wherein the trivalent chromium coating solution comprises a fluoride compound. 13. The method of claim 1 wherein the trivalent chromium coating solution comprises a fiuorozirconate compound. 14. The method of claim 1 wherein the trivalent chromium coating solution comprises a chelating agent. 15. A trivalent chromium coating solution produced by the process of claim 1. 16. A trivalent chromium coating solution comprising: trivalent chromium; water; and a corrosion inhibitor compound; wherein the trivalent chromium coating solution has a pH of approximately 3.5 to approximately 4.0; and wherein the pH of the trivalent chromium coating solution varies no more than + 0.15 while in storage for at least 7 days. 17. The trivalent chromium coating solution of claim 16 wherein the trivalent chromium coating solution is an aqueous trivalent chromium coating solution. 18. The trivalent chromium coating solution of claim 16 comprising trivalent chromium sulfate. 19. The trivalent chromium coating solution of claim 16 comprising a fluoride compound. 20. The trivalent chromium coating solution of claim 16 comprising a fiuorozirconate compound. 21. The trivalent chromium coating solution of claim 16 comprising a chelating agent. 22. The trivalent chromium coating solution of claim 21 wherein the chelating agent comprises 8-hydroxyquinoline and/or nitrilotris(methylene)triphosphonic acid. 23. The trivalent chromium coating solution of claim 16 wherein the pH of the trivalent chromium coating solution varies no more than Â± 0.07 while in storage for at least 7 days. 24. The t ivalent chromium coating solution of claim 16 wherein the corrosion inhibitor comprises 2-mercaptobenzothiazole, 2-mercaptobenzimidazole, 2-mercaptobenzoxazole, and/or benzotriazole. 25. A trivalent chromium coating solution comprising: trivalent chromium sulfate; and water; wherein the trivalent chromium coating solution has a pH of approximately 3.5 to approximately 4.0; wherein the pH of the trivalent chromium coating solution varies no more than + 0.15 while in storage for at least 7 days. 26. The trivalent chromium coating solution of claim 25 comprising a fluoride compound. 27. The trivalent chromium coating solution of claim 25 comprising a fluorozirconate compound. 28. The trivalent chromium coating solution of claim 25 comprising a chelating agent. 29. A heat stabilized trivalent chromium coating solution. 30. The heat stabilized trivalent chromium coating solution of claim 29 wherein the pH of the heat stabilized trivalent chromium coating solution varies no more than + 0.15 while in storage for at least 7 days.
